title: iOS 19 Expected to Run on These iPhones
content:
iOS 19 will not be available on the iPhone XR, ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XS, or the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XS Max, according a private account on social media site X that has accurately provided information on device compatibility in the past.


The ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XR, ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XS, and ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XS Max all have an A12 Bionic chip, so it looks like ‚ÄåiOS 19‚Äå will discontinue support for that chip. All other iPhones that run iOS 18 are expected to support ‚ÄåiOS 19‚Äå, with a full compatibility list below.

  • iPhone 16e

  • iPhone 16

  • ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå Plus

  • iPhone 16 Pro

  • ‚ÄåiPhone 16 Pro‚Äå Max

  • iPhone 15

  • ‚ÄåiPhone 15‚Äå Plus

  • ‚ÄåiPhone 15‚Äå Pro

  • ‚ÄåiPhone 15‚Äå Pro Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 14

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 14 Plus

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 14 Pro

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 14 Pro Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 13

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 13 mini

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 13 Pro

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 13 Pro Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 12

  • iPhone 12 mini

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 12 Pro

  • iPhone 12 Pro Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 11

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 11 Pro

  • iPhone 11 Pro Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå SE (2nd generation or later)


While these iPhones will support ‚ÄåiOS 19‚Äå, advanced features may be limited to newer iPhones with faster processors. Apple Intelligence in ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå, for example, only runs on the ‚ÄåiPhone 15‚Äå Pro and the ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå models.

As for iPadOS 19, it is expected to drop support for the seventh-generation low-cost iPad with A10 Fusion chip. It sounds like iPads that are equipped with the A12 Bionic such as the iPad mini 5 will be compatible with iPadOS 19.

The source that shared the information also provided details on the devices that would be compatible with ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå ahead of when ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå was unveiled.

Apple is planning to introduce ‚ÄåiOS 19‚Äå and iPadOS 19 at the Worldwide Developers Conference that is set to begin on Monday, June 9.
